overall_summary: Big gap to reference, but the data shows exactly where it is hiding. Attack these points. Current gap: 64.964s slower than reference. The biggest opportunity is Sector 1 (+50.001s), where 74.6 km/h is being left on the table through the corners. Executing these fixes could recover 2.11s — a completely different lap time.

priority_actions.issue: Apex speed is 37.3 km/h below reference (57.1 vs 94.4 km/h). Significant corner exit speed deficit.
priority_actions.instruction: Despite a late brake point, apex speed is below reference — car not rotating. Use higher initial brake pressure to pitch the car in, trail off through apex. Hit 94 km/h and the exit speed takes care of itself.
priority_actions.evidence: Apex: 57.1 km/h vs 94.4 km/h reference (-37.3 km/h).

priority_actions.issue: Apex speed is 23.8 km/h below reference (41.6 vs 65.5 km/h). Significant corner exit speed deficit.
priority_actions.instruction: Despite a late brake point, apex speed is below reference — car not rotating. Use higher initial brake pressure to pitch the car in, trail off through apex. Hit 66 km/h and the exit speed takes care of itself.
priority_actions.evidence: Apex: 41.6 km/h vs 65.5 km/h reference (-23.8 km/h).

priority_actions.issue: Throttle pick-up is 70m later than reference after the apex.
priority_actions.instruction: Get back to full throttle 70m earlier. Reference applies throttle at 2710m, driver at 2780m. Trust the apex and commit — late throttle bleeds straight-line speed.

priority_actions.issue: Throttle pick-up is 40m later than reference after the apex.
priority_actions.instruction: Get back to full throttle 40m earlier. Reference applies throttle at 1525m, driver at 1565m. Trust the apex and commit — late throttle bleeds straight-line speed.
